revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:尼崎戎神社]]
{{Infobox religious building |name = Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine |image = File:2015 1024 Amaebisu Haiden.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Amagasaki|en|Amagasaki|qid=Q213318|lt=Amagasaki|1=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|Kanda Nakadori|ja|神田中通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Nakadori|1=Kanda Nakadori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131932332}} 3-28 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|10.6|N|135|24|45.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = Tradition: Before the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|qid=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} (885 AD) |本殿 = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style, {{ill|Hipped Roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hipped Roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q90373477}} (Cypress Bark Roofing) |別名 = Kuramochi Ebisu Ōmiya, "Chijimi-san" |札所等 = |festival = January 9-11 |神事 = Tōka Ebisu (Ten-day Ebisu Festival) |地図={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=10.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=24|lon_sec=45.7|label=Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|10.6|N|135|24|45.7|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Amagasaki|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.719611|N|135.412694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{Osm box|w|293510429|qq=}} '''Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine''' (尼崎戎神社, ''Amagasaki Ebisu Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Amagasaki|en|Amagasaki|qid=Q213318|lt=Amagasaki|1=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. It is also known as '''Kuramochi Ebisu Ōmiya''' or colloquially as "'''Chijimi-san'''" (see Facilities section below)<ref name="apedia">[http://www.archives.city.amagasaki.hyogo.jp/apedia/index.php?key=%E6%88%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE Ebisu Shrine] - Amagasaki City Regional Research Archive, January 11, 2007</ref>. The shrine is popular among worshippers as "'''Nani no Ebessan'''" (尼のえべっさん), a deity of commercial prosperity, and hosts the grand '''Tōka Ebisu''' festival every year from January 9 to 11. == History == [[File:2015 1024 Amaebisu Shagohyo.jpg|thumb|200px|"Kotoshironushi Shrine" marker within the shrine grounds]] Tradition holds that the shrine was established before the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|qid=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} (885 AD)<ref>[http://www.genki.or.jp/wp/archives/1899 Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine] - Genki Machi</ref>. During the {{ill|Shōwa (1312–1317)|en|Shōwa (1312–1317)|qid=Q1324807|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa (1312–1317)}} (1312-1317), a shrine affiliated with {{ill|Hiyoshi Shrine|ja|日吉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505065}} in Omi Sakamoto was enshrined in Amagasaki. It later split into separate shrines in Bessho Town and Bessho Village. The shrine in Bessho Village, located at Uchihata (now Nishihonmachi 5-chome, Amagasaki City), became known as '''Ebisu Taijingū''', serving as the local {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) shrine<ref group="note">The Bessho Town branch, "Hatsushima Ebisu," later relocated to Tsukiji Town and became the present-day {{ill|Hatsushima Daijingū|ja|初嶋大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushima Daijingū|qq=}}.</ref><ref name=apedia/>. In the modern era, Ebisu Taijingū was renamed '''Kotoshironushi Shrine''' and later changed to '''Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine'''. During World War II in 1945, the shrine grounds were designated for house evacuation, but they were spared due to the war's end<ref name=apedia/>. === Timeline === * 1662 (Kanbun 2): Ebisu Taijingū established in Bessho Village Uchihata (now Amagasaki) * 1873 (Meiji 6), August: Designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} * 1948 (Shōwa 23): Renamed to Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine * 1953 (Shōwa 28): Relocated to current site in Kanda Nakadori 3-chome * 1958 (Shōwa 33): Great Torii Gate constructed, completed in June the following year * 2013 (Heisei 25), January: New shrine office constructed == Deities == Historically, the shrine was near the sea and Kotoshironushi, who was associated with fishing and navigation, was worshipped as the deity. Over time, the deity became revered as a patron of commercial prosperity. Additionally, a legend states that {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, on his way to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|qid=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}}, stopped by Amagasaki and was so impressed by the old pines and coastal scenery that the area was named "'''Koto-no-Ura'''" (琴の浦, "Harp Bay") after his poetic description<ref group="note">Sugawara no Michizane is said to have remarked, "This is an especially beautiful bay. The pines are lined as if forming a harp."</ref><ref name="尼崎市あんない"/>. As a result, he is enshrined as a deity of scholarship (Tenjin-sama). == Festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (New Year's Festival) * January 9: Eve of Ebisu * January 10: Main Ebisu Festival * January 11: Remaining Ebisu Festival * February 3: {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} Festival * April 15: Kōhō-in Inari Shrine Festival * July 9-10: Summer Purification Festival * October - November 30: {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|qid=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebrations * December 31: New Year's Eve Festival == Grounds == [[File:2015 1024 Amaebisu Honden.jpg|thumb|200px|Honden housed inside a protective structure]] [[File:2015 1024 Amaebisu Otorii.jpg|thumb|200px|Great Torii Gate]] The shrine is known locally as "Chijimi-san" due to its history of suffering war damage, disasters, and reductions in size over the centuries<ref>[http://goshuinshuushuu.blog.fc2.com/blog-category-52.html Kuramochi Ebisu Ōmiya (Hyōgo, Amagasaki) - Amagasaki Ebisu Shrine] Goshuin Collection, October 2, 2012</ref>. The shrine grounds form a narrow triangle with structures mainly on the western side. The large Torii Gate stands centrally, with the surrounding area open as a public park called '''Kanda Park'''. == External Links == * [http://www.amaebisu.com/ Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/amaebisu Blog by Shrine Priest Nobby] <references /> <references group="note"/>
{{translated page|ja|尼崎戎神社|version=101720155|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:尼崎戎神社]] [[ja:%E5%B0%BC%E5%B4%8E%E6%88%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]
